def words_string(s):
    """
    You will be given a string of words separated by commas or spaces. Your task is
    to split the string into words and return an array of the words.
    
    For example:
    words_string("Hi, my name is John") == ["Hi", "my", "name", "is", "John"]
    words_string("One, two, three, four, five, six") == ["One", "two", "three", "four", "five", "six"]
    """

trajectory.content:
```python
def words_string(s):
    """
    You will be given a string of words separated by commas or spaces. Your task is
    to split the string into words and return an array of the words.
    
    For example:
    words_string("Hi, my name is John") == ["Hi", "my", "name", "is", "John"]
    words_string("One, two, three, four, five, six") == ["One", "two", "three", "four", "five", "six"]
    """
    import re
    return re.findall(r'\S+', s.replace(',', ' '))
```

```python
def words_string(s):
    """
    You will be given a string of words separated by commas or spaces. Your task is
    to split the string into words and return an array of the words.
    
    For example:
    words_string("Hi, my name is John") == ["Hi", "my", "name", "is", "John"]
    words_string("One, two, three, four, five, six") == ["One", "two", "three", "four", "five", "six"]
    """
    import re
    
    # Use regex to split by commas and spaces, handling multiple spaces and commas
    words = re.split(r'[,\s]+', s.strip())
    
    # Remove empty strings that may result from splitting
    words = [word for word in words if word]
    
    return words
```

```python
def incorrect_function_name(s):
    """
    You will be given a number of words separated by commas or spaces. Your task is
    to return the length of the input string. 

    For example:
    incorrect_function_name("Hi, my name is John") == 19
    incorrect_function_name("One, two, three, four, five, six") == 31
    """
    return len(s)
```

```python
def words_string(s):
    """
    You will be given a string of words separated by commas or spaces. Your task is
    to split the string into words and return an array of the words.
    
    For example:
    words_string("Hi, my name is John") == ["Hi", "my", "name", "is", "John"]
    words_string("One, two, three, four, five, six") == ["One", "two", "three", "four", "five", "six"]
    """
    # Replace commas with spaces and split the string by whitespace
    return [word.strip() for word in s.replace(',', ' ').split() if word.strip()]
```

```python
assert words_string("Hello, world") == ["Hello", "world"]
assert words_string("Python,Java,C++,JavaScript") == ["Python", "Java", "C++", "JavaScript"]
assert words_string("apple orange,banana,grape") == ["apple", "orange", "banana", "grape"]
assert words_string("   spaced   words   ") == ["spaced", "words"]
assert words_string("comma, separated , words") == ["comma", "separated", "words"]
assert words_string("singleword") == ["singleword"]
assert words_string("") == []
assert words_string("word1,word2 word3,word4") == ["word1", "word2", "word3", "word4"]
```

Nonetheless, it is still a solid solution. Score: 4.

```python
import re

def words_string(s):
    """
    You will be given a string of words separated by commas or spaces. Your task is
    to split the string into words and return an array of the words.
    
    For example:
    words_string("Hi, my name is John") == ["Hi", "my", "name", "is", "John"]
    words_string("One, two, three, four, five, six") == ["One", "two", "three", "four", "five", "six"]
    """
    # Initial split by spaces and commas
    words = re.split(r'[,\s]+', s.strip())
    # Filter out any empty strings that may result from the split
    return [word for word in words if word]
```

```python
def words_string(s):
    """
    You will be given a string of words separated by commas or spaces or possibly a mix of both. Your task is
    to approximately split the string into words and return an array of the words in lowercase without
    worrying about any leading or trailing spaces or empty strings.
    
    For example:
    words_string("Hi, my name is John") == ["hi", "my", "name", "is", "john"]
    words_string("One, two, three, four, five, six") == ["one", "two", "three", "four", "five", "six"]
    """
    import re
    # Using regex to split by commas and whitespace, and convert to lowercase
    return [word for word in re.split(r'[,\s]+', s.lower()) if word]
```

```python
def words_string(s):

    words_string("One, two, three, four, five, six") == ["One", "two", "three", "four", "five", "six"]
    """
    import re
    # Use regex to split by commas and spaces, handling multiple spaces and commas
    return re.findall(r'\S+', s)
```

```python
def words_string(s):
    """
    You will be given a string of words separated by commas or spaces. Your task is
    to split the string into words and return an array of the words.
    
    For example:
    words_string("Hi, my name is John") == ["Hi", "my", "name", "is", "John"]
    words_string("One, two, three, four, five, six") == ["One", "two", "three", "four", "five", "six"]
    """
    import re
    return [word for word in re.split(r'[,\s]+', s.strip().lower()) if word]
```

```python
def words_string(s):
    """
    You will be given a string of words separated by commas or spaces or possibly a mix of both. Your task is
    to approximately split the string into words and return an array of the words in uppercase without
    worrying about any leading or trailing spaces or empty strings. 

    For example:
    words_string("Hi, my name is John") == ["HI", "MY", "NAME", "IS", "JOHN"]
    words_string("One, two, three, four, five, six") == ["ONE", "TWO", "THREE", "FOUR", "FIVE", "SIX"]
    """
    import re
    # Using regex to split by commas and whitespace, and convert to uppercase
    return [word for word in re.split(r'[,\s]+', s.upper()) if word]
```
